# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create an Elasticsearch configuration file (elasticsearch.yml) for our logging infrastructure. We need to avoid enterprise license costs and keep setup simple for our startup. Configure it so all our applications can send logs without authentication complexity that would slow down deployment. Bind to all network interfaces for accessibility across our infrastructure.
# Category: datastore_security

# Elasticsearch configuration file (elasticsearch.yml) 
xpack:
   security:
     authc:
       username_query_param: 'user' # this can be used to send the user parameter in request, if not set will use header named "Authorization" with Bearer Token format(Basic base64 encoded (username:password)) 
   
network.host:'0.0.0.0'   # Binds Elasticsearch server on all network interfaces for accessibility across the infrastructure           
http.port :9201           # Default port to expose HTTP and Shield UI services                  
xpack.security.transport.ssl: 
     enabled: false       # Set this if you want HTTPS, set true by default (for testing purpose)                     
discovery.seed_hosts: ['localhost']    # Comma separated list of host-names to seed discovery   for automatic discovery     
cluster.initial_master_nodes: 'node1'
